telltalePatterns: vowel reduction absent in unstressed syllables
lenition of labiodental fricative /v/ to bilabial [β]

prosody.intonation: Slightly flat compared to native English speakers, lacking wide pitch variation on primarily stressed words.
prosody.fluencyNotes: Very fluent with smooth transitions and minimal hesitation; good connected speech but lacks native-like elision.

strengths: Excellent fluency and speech pacing without unnatural pauses
Strong grammatical control and confident delivery

recommendedFocus.reason: Implementing proper vowel reduction will help shift the rhythm from syllable-timed to stress-timed, drastically improving naturalness.
Distinguishing between these vowels prevents minimal pair confusion (e.g. 'lives' sounding like 'leaves').
Articulating /v/ with the top teeth and bottom lip will clear up the slight bilabial distortion.

result.transcript: It's funny how some people always seem to have their lives together, well I'm just trying to find my matching socks.
